parentQuestion: Dual innervation means that most visceral organs receive input from ___.

scaffolds.q: The sympathetic and parasympathetic divisions often have opposite effects.
scaffolds.explain: Sympathetic speeds up the heart; parasympathetic slows it. Sympathetic dilates bronchi; parasympathetic constricts them. this opposition creates a spectrum of control, not just on/off. The balance between divisions determines the final state. Exquisite modulation.

scaffolds.q: The ANS maintains homeostasis by adjusting visceral organ function.
scaffolds.explain: Homeostasis requires constant adjustment — body temperature, blood pressure, digestive activity all fluctuate. Enter this realm:: the ANS is the primary effector system that makes these adjustments, shifting the balance between sympathetic and parasympathetic as needs change. Immerse yourself in this:the dynamic equilibrium.
